zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Beauchamp於1381年2月5日出生在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾沃里克沃里克 卡素時，他的父親William de Beauchamp38歲，母親Joan FitzAlan14歲。他於大約1398年9月與Elizabeth de Berkeley在英國結婚。他們至少是4個女兒的父母。他於1408年登記服兵役。他去世於1439年5月9日在法國瑟-易費利額盧享年58歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾沃里克聖 馬也斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Beauchamp于1381年2月5日出生在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾沃里克沃里克 卡素时，他的父亲William de Beauchamp38岁，母亲Joan FitzAlan14岁。他于大约1398年9月与Elizabeth de Berkeley在英国结婚。他们至少是4个女儿的父母。他于1408年登记服兵役。他去世于1439年5月9日在法国瑟-易費利額盧享年58岁，埋葬在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾沃里克聖 馬也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Beauchamp was born on 5 February 1381, in Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, William de Beauchamp, was 38 and his mother, Joan FitzAlan, was 14. He married Elizabeth de Berkeley about September 1398, in England. They were the parents of at least 4 daughters. He registered for military service in 1408. He died on 9 May 1439, in Rouen, Seine-Inférieure, France, at the age of 58, and was buried in St Mary's Church, Warwick, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Beauchamp est né le 5 février 1381, à Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William de Beauchamp, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Joan FitzAlan, avait 14 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth de Berkeley environ septembre 1398, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1408. Il est décédé le 9 mai 1439, à Rouen, Seine-Inférieure, France, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterré à St Mary's Church, Warwick, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Beauchamp am 5. Februar 1381 in Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Beauchamp 38 und seine Mutter Joan FitzAlan 14. Er hat um September 1398 in England Elizabeth de Berkeley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Töchter. Er hat sich 1408 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb am 9. Mai 1439 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Rouen, Seine-Inférieure, Frankreich und wurde in St Mary's Church, Warwick, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Beauchamp è nato il il 5 febbraio 1381 nel Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William de Beauchamp, aveva 38 anni e il suo madre, Joan FitzAlan, aveva 14 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth de Berkeley circa settembre 1398, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1408. Morì il 9 maggio 1439, nel Rouen, Seine-Inférieure, Francia, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolto a St Mary's Church, Warwick, Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Beauchampが1381年2月5日にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアワーウィックワーウィック カストルで生まれた時，父William de Beauchampは38歳，母Joan FitzAlanは14歳であった。約1398年9月にイギリスでElizabeth de Berkeleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の娘の親であった。彼は1408年に兵役登録をした。彼は1439年5月9日にフランスセアイネ-インフeリーウアロウンで58歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアワーウィックスト マリ'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Beauchamp(이)가 1381년 2월 5일 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 워윅 워윅 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Beauchamp(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan FitzAlan(은)는 14세였다. 그는 대략 1398년 9월 영국에서 Elizabeth de Berkeley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1408년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1439년 5월 9일 프랑스 세이인-인프e리우르 루언에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 워윅 스트 마이'스 처르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Beauchamp nasceu em 5 de fevereiro de 1381, em Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William de Beauchamp, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Joan FitzAlan, tinha 14 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth de Berkeley aproximadamente setembro de 1398, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1408. Ele faleceu em 9 de maio de 1439, em Rouen, Seine-Inférieure, França, com 58 anos, e foi sepultado em St Mary's Church, Warwick, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Beauchamp родился 5 февраля 1381 г. в местности варвикк кастл, варвикк, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William de Beauchamp было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan FitzAlan, 14. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth de Berkeley около сентябрь 1398 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 дочери. В 1408 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 9 мая 1439 г. в местности роуен, сине-инфeриеур, Франция в возрасте 58 лет и был похоронен в местности ст мэри'с чурч, варвикк, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Beauchamp nació el 5 de febrero de 1381, en Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William de Beauchamp, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Joan FitzAlan, tenía 14 años. Se casó con Elizabeth de Berkeley aproximadamente septiembre de 1398, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1408. Murió el 9 de mayo de 1439, en Rouen, Seine-Inférieure, Francia, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultado en St Mary's Church, Warwick, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
